insight: Bosnia-Herzegovina's performance after hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows a mixed trend, with the team gaining momentum in only half of the breaks, resulting in a slight decline in performance on average. When they were pressing before a break, they tended to lose momentum afterwards, with their performance declining at a rate of 0.200 units per minute, which could indicate that the break disrupts their rhythm and intensity. In contrast, when they were not pressing before a break, their performance remained relatively stable, with no significant change in momentum, suggesting that the break may have allowed them to regroup and maintain their level of play. Overall, the data suggests that hydration breaks can have a negative impact on Bosnia-Herzegovina's performance, particularly when they are in an aggressive pressing mode.
